# GateIo.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the Gate.io REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports Spot, Margin, Unified account, Perpetual Futures, Alpha API, Rebate partner endpoints, and WebSocket subscriptions. Part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem providing a unified abstraction across many cryptocurrency exchanges.

GateIo.Net provides typed models, automatic WebSocket reconnection, comprehensive error handling via the `WebCallResult<T>` / `CallResult<T>` pattern, client-side rate limiting, local order book support, native AOT support, and SharedApis integration. Current version in this repository: 3.10.2. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/README.md): Overview, installation, supported features, basic usage examples, full release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/GateIo.Net/): Full documentation hub including client reference, WebSocket subscriptions, dependency injection, and shared interfaces
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/docs/ai-api-map.md): Quick intent-to-method map for routing common user requests to the correct client member
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI/LLM guide with code patterns, endpoint map, common mistakes, and anti-hallucination rules
- [Assistant Skill](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): Compact skill file with GateIo.Net conventions and examples

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/tree/main/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact, fully runnable examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker, account balance, limit order placement, order status check
- [Perpetual Futures](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs): Perpetual futures with settlement asset, contract lookup, leverage, order placement, position retrieval
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Ticker subscription, kline subscription, authenticated spot updates, futures public stream
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): Using CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis for exchange-agnostic code
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): WebCallResult pattern, retry logic, common Gate.io routing mistakes and validation checks
- [Full Examples Repository](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/tree/main/Examples): Additional examples for the library

## Critical Usage Rules

- Use `GateIoRestClient` for REST and `GateIoSocketClient` for WebSocket. Do not generate raw HTTP calls.
- Use `GateIoCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`.
- Native Gate.io symbols use underscores, for example `ETH_USDT`.
- Perpetual futures methods require a settlement asset such as `"usdt"` plus a contract such as `"ETH_USDT"`.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- For futures orders, `quantity` is an `int` number of contracts, not a decimal base asset amount.
- For multi-exchange code, use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` via `.SharedClient`.

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net base library](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net): Base abstractions used by GateIo.Net and other exchange libraries
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net): Bundle package providing unified access to all exchange libraries
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/GateIo.Net): Latest stable release on NuGet

- [Discord Community](https://discord.gg/MSpeEtSY8t): Maintainer-supported Discord for questions and discussion
- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/JKorf/GateIo.Net/issues): Bug reports and feature requests
